title: Nev-AD-a

jokes.text: I'm having reservations about the reservations.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether to include Native American precincts in the recount
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'reservations' - both doubt and Native American land
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's perfectly economic and contextually appropriate. The double meaning lands cleanly.

jokes.text: I'm meeting troops and shaking God only knows what.
jokes.setup: Selina describing her visit to Walter Reed hospital
jokes.punchline: Implication that she's disgusted by touching wounded veterans
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's callous character perfectly - turning a hospital visit into a complaint about hygiene

jokes.text: She's changed her mind more times than a frickin' child molester at Disneyland.
jokes.setup: Amy complaining about Selina's constant precinct changes
jokes.punchline: Extremely dark and inappropriate comparison to a child molester
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark humor that's memorable but relies heavily on transgression for impact

jokes.text: find a rattlesnake and shove that up your dick hole 'cause it's a lot more fun than what I'll do to you.
jokes.setup: Amy threatening consequences for missing the filing deadline
jokes.punchline: Escalating from violent imagery to something even worse
jokes.explanation: Typical Veep escalation but the imagery is more graphic than clever

jokes.text: Maddox is probably going to study the effects of legalized prostitution on his dick.
jokes.setup: Maddox flying to Nevada
jokes.punchline: Implying he'll use his trip as an excuse to visit prostitutes
jokes.explanation: Predictable sex joke but delivers economically and fits the character dynamics

jokes.text: maybe we can get some six-year-old Asian kid to fix it.
jokes.setup: Technical difficulties with video conference
jokes.punchline: Racist stereotype about Asian technical ability
jokes.explanation: Lazy stereotypical humor that relies on offensive assumptions rather than wit

jokes.text: We're gonna name her Ellen after Wendy's mom. Actually, her stepmom. She has a difficult relation...
jokes.setup: Mike sharing personal information about his baby
jokes.punchline: Trailing off as he realizes no one cares about his oversharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment - oversharing personal details to people who don't care

jokes.text: misunderstood your level of interest, sorry.
jokes.setup: Mike's oversharing was cut off
jokes.punchline: His apologetic acknowledgment that no one wanted to hear it
jokes.explanation: Perfectly deadpan Mike line that shows self-awareness of his social awkwardness

jokes.text: Seriously, I'm more Hispanic than she is. You know? Where's she from, Santo Connecticut?
jokes.setup: Complaining about Montez's Hispanic credentials
jokes.punchline: Made-up Hispanic-sounding version of Connecticut
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke that shows Selina's pettiness and competitive nature

jokes.text: She summers in Vinyardo Del Martha.
jokes.setup: Following up on Selina's Santo Connecticut joke
jokes.punchline: Hispanicizing Martha's Vineyard
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds on Selina's joke with Ben's dry delivery

jokes.text: She's from El New Hampshire.
jokes.setup: Continuing the Hispanic place name joke
jokes.punchline: Adding 'El' to New Hampshire
jokes.explanation: Weaker callback - more predictable than Ben's version

jokes.text: Harpo, Chico, and Shito.
jokes.setup: Asking what team she has versus O'Brien's Secretary of State
jokes.punchline: Marx Brothers reference but replacing Groucho with 'Shito'
jokes.explanation: Clever reference that shows Selina's view of her team while playing on classic comedy

jokes.text: Maybe they can find out what some of these people are doing.
jokes.setup: Chinese hackers have breached White House employee files
jokes.punchline: Suggesting even she doesn't know what her staff does
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina line showing her disconnection from her own administration

jokes.text: This Olympic-size swimming pool of shit that I'm doing the backstroke in right now?
jokes.setup: Asked to thank campaign volunteers
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor for her current predicament
jokes.explanation: Vivid, specific imagery that perfectly captures Selina's dramatic self-pity

jokes.text: You are looking at a guy on the master cleanse. I feel amazing.
jokes.setup: Mike declining breakfast
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's on a ridiculous diet trend
jokes.explanation: Sets up Mike's running gag about his ridiculous diet, perfect character moment

jokes.text: What in the lunatic fuck is the master cleanse? Sounds like Nazi domestic policy.
jokes.setup: Mike mentions the master cleanse
jokes.punchline: Comparing diet trend to Nazi policies
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that's unexpected and fits Ben's cynical worldview

jokes.text: Little known fact about the Nazis... their polling numbers within Germany, through the roof. Unbelievable numbers. Though also tragic.
jokes.setup: Following Ben's Nazi reference
jokes.punchline: Kent's analytical approach to Nazi popularity statistics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent moment - turning genocide into polling data with casual afterthought about tragedy

jokes.text: The Jewish demo couldn't have been that good.
jokes.setup: Kent discussing Nazi polling
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about Holocaust victims
jokes.explanation: Continues Kent's analytical detachment but pushes into uncomfortable territory

jokes.text: Not exactly take.
jokes.setup: Asked if he still takes shits on his cleanse
jokes.punchline: Implying he has no control over his bowel movements
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical gross-out humor that develops Mike's diet storyline

jokes.text: Or you could email me at splett2@splettnet.net. Splett1 is my father. It'll be sad to see him go, but it'll be nice to get my hands on that handle, you know?
jokes.setup: Richard offering to email election law information
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of waiting for his father to die for an email address
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Richard moment - mixing casual inheritance planning with email logistics in the most inappropriate way

jokes.text: What is wrong with you, you Paddington Bear-looking fuck?
jokes.setup: Richard just gave away strategic information
jokes.punchline: Specific visual insult comparing Richard to children's character
jokes.explanation: Specific, unexpected visual comparison that's both insulting and oddly accurate

jokes.text: You guys have Michael Jordan sitting on the bench here, but you're starting Hakeem Olajutwat.
jokes.setup: Jonah complaining about not being used effectively
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing Hakeem Olajuwon's name as profanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah combination of self-aggrandizement and casual mispronunciation

jokes.text: You are a seven-foot-seven goony-looking Lithuanian who's gonna drop dead of Marfan syndrome.
jokes.setup: Amy responding to Jonah's basketball metaphor
jokes.punchline: Specific medical condition based on his height and appearance
jokes.explanation: Medically specific insult that escalates from sports metaphor to death prediction

jokes.text: Plus I would never miss the opportunity to dry fuck Jim Whitman up the ass.
jokes.setup: Bradley explaining why he's helping Selina
jokes.punchline: Graphic sexual metaphor for defeating opponent
jokes.explanation: Shows Bradley's crude old-school political style but relies on shock value

jokes.text: All right, let's hear it, Affirmative Action Jackson.
jokes.setup: Richard about to share information about mail-in ballots
jokes.punchline: Racially insensitive nickname for Richard
jokes.explanation: Shows Bradley's old-school inappropriate style but relies on offensive stereotypes

jokes.text: Well, Madam President, I've got big balls, but neither one of them are crystal.
jokes.setup: Selina asking if they'll win the recount
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'crystal ball' fortune telling while referencing anatomy
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels with confident delivery

jokes.text: Henry, you son of a bitch. I can lead a horse to water, but I can't milk it.
jokes.setup: Bradley telling a story about Nixon and Kissinger
jokes.punchline: Completely mangled version of common sayings
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd mixing of sayings that sounds profound but makes no sense

jokes.text: Can't milk it! I don't even know what that means.
jokes.setup: Bradley's nonsensical Nixon quote
jokes.punchline: Selina's honest admission that it makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction that acknowledges the absurdity

jokes.text: We called him Fatty Dicksuckle and B-B-Benny and the Jizz. Buttfucker.
jokes.setup: Bradley reminiscing about Ben's intern days
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude and inappropriate nicknames
jokes.explanation: Shows Bradley's crude style but relies more on shock than craft

jokes.text: Buttfucker, that's me. I can't believe you remembered my nickname.
jokes.setup: Bradley listing Ben's offensive nicknames
jokes.punchline: Ben's deadpan acceptance and false gratitude
jokes.explanation: Ben's deadpan response to humiliation shows perfect character consistency

jokes.text: It says 'Fuck Selina Meyer.' If anything, that one should count for us.
jokes.setup: Examining a ballot with profanity about Selina
jokes.punchline: Arguing that insults should count as votes
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd campaign logic that any attention equals votes

jokes.text: So it doesn't say 'Fuck Selina Meyer.' It says, you know, 'Fuck, Selina Meyer!' That's a testament to this voter's earthy but unambiguous enthusiasm for President Meyer.
jokes.setup: Amy suggesting the ballot should count for them
jokes.punchline: Richard's elaborate grammatical argument for counting a 'fuck you' vote
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that shows Richard's earnest stupidity and creative interpretation

jokes.text: I'm sure the Dow is rocketing skywards as we speak.
jokes.setup: Selina turning his policy meeting into a photo op
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about the economic impact
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcasm showing Tom's frustration with Selina's priorities

jokes.text: I'm gonna be pulling down Clint Eastwood cowboy movie style pussy in these things. Except I'm not the man with no name. I'm the man with mo' game.
jokes.setup: Jonah showing off his new boots
jokes.punchline: Elaborate sexual boast with Clint Eastwood reference and wordplay
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah over-elaboration that shows his delusional confidence

jokes.text: Afternoon, sir. We're here because your ballot was disqualified because your signature didn't match... okay, well, fooled me with that one.
jokes.setup: Amy approaching what she thinks is a voter
jokes.punchline: Realizing she's talking to a statue or mannequin
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment showing the stress and confusion of the campaign

jokes.text: Can Paul McCartney teach Kid Rock how to be a good songwriter? I mean, that's a bad example. Kid Rock rules, but...
jokes.setup: Richard explaining why he can't teach his methods
jokes.punchline: Terrible analogy followed by immediate contradiction of his own point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard moment - starting with pseudo-wisdom and immediately undermining himself

jokes.text: A bit of an asshat? Actually, I was gonna say a champion for the middle class, but I kind of like your thing better to tell you the truth.
jokes.setup: Banker calling Tom an asshat
jokes.punchline: Selina pretending she was going to defend him but agreeing instead
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's willingness to throw Tom under the bus for banker approval

jokes.text: Two grand? Where'd you find that? In your sofa seat cushion?
jokes.setup: Banker mentions donating to her campaign
jokes.punchline: Mocking the small donation amount
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's casual dismissal of regular people's financial contributions

jokes.text: I think it was in my other pants, Madam President.
jokes.setup: Selina mocking his small donation
jokes.punchline: Humble, self-deprecating response to her insult
jokes.explanation: Charming comeback that deflects Selina's rudeness with humor

jokes.text: Not in jail for what she did to you.
jokes.setup: Asking about someone's ex-wife
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate comment about the divorce
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's lack of social filter when trying to be sympathetic

jokes.text: Brooks Brothers sews prenups into all our suits.
jokes.setup: Discussion of divorces among wealthy bankers
jokes.punchline: Suggesting prenups are standard equipment for rich men
jokes.explanation: Clever line about wealth and marriage protection that's economically delivered

jokes.text: Including our daughter.
jokes.setup: Saying divorce was the best thing she and husband ever did
jokes.punchline: Including having their child as lesser achievement
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about priorities that lands with perfect timing and economy

jokes.text: some of your economic policies are what my friend Katie Gross likes to call fakakta.
jokes.setup: Criticizing Selina's economic policies
jokes.punchline: Using Yiddish slang for 'fucked up'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Katie Gross connection while delivering policy criticism

jokes.text: Yeah, so did I. 'Cause I was at Smith. No, it's a Smith joke.
jokes.setup: Selina says she dated Katie Gross at Smith
jokes.punchline: Explaining it's a lesbian joke about women's college
jokes.explanation: Academic in-joke about women's colleges and lesbian experimentation

jokes.text: Sorry. You should read C.S. Lewis's book on grief. It's not as fun as 'The Lion, Witch, and the Wardrobe' series, but it's still pretty good.
jokes.setup: Learning a voter has died
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate book recommendation comparing grief literature to children's fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard moment - inappropriate sincerity mixed with literary criticism

jokes.text: Alav hashalom. Hebrew.
jokes.setup: Someone mentioning death
jokes.punchline: Jonah showing off his Hebrew knowledge inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah inappropriate showing off that misses the social context

jokes.text: Well, I just came out West to play a little Cowboys and Injuns with Big Chief Suckum Choad here.
jokes.setup: Furlong explaining his presence in Nevada
jokes.punchline: Racist game reference with crude nickname for Richard
jokes.explanation: Shows Furlong's offensive character but relies on crude stereotypes

jokes.text: How. Me suck pee pee in teepee.
jokes.setup: Furlong making Richard say racist Native American stereotypes
jokes.punchline: Richard's humiliating compliance with crude language
jokes.explanation: Shows Richard's degradation but uncomfortable humor relying on racist tropes

jokes.text: The other person in the room who looks exactly like Grimace.
jokes.setup: Furlong addressing someone in purple
jokes.punchline: Comparing them to McDonald's character Grimace
jokes.explanation: Specific visual insult that's mean but effectively descriptive

jokes.text: All right? It's got nothing to do with your body shape or your... the way your head goes into your shoulders so smoothly.
jokes.setup: Furlong claiming his insult wasn't about appearance
jokes.punchline: Immediately describing exactly the physical traits he was mocking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Furlong's inability to be diplomatic even when trying

jokes.text: I don't care for movies.
jokes.setup: Someone mentioning The Godfather
jokes.punchline: Kent's complete dismissal of an entire art form
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent line - completely unexpected and delivered with absolute certainty

jokes.text: It's bronze. Oh. Oh, bronze medal.
jokes.setup: Selina congratulating athlete on gold medal
jokes.punchline: Correction to bronze, followed by Selina's awkward repetition
jokes.explanation: Classic political photo-op awkwardness showing Selina's lack of preparation

jokes.text: Fuck am I supposed to do with this?
jokes.setup: Receiving a kayak from the bronze medalist
jokes.punchline: Selina's blunt assessment of a useless gift
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the absurdity of ceremonial gifts and political obligations

jokes.text: I just sometimes need a little banking task force.
jokes.setup: Selina wanting another banking meeting
jokes.punchline: Describing policy meetings like an addiction or sexual need
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre that works as both policy discussion and sexual euphemism

jokes.text: Well, ma'am, if you want, I can arrange a more discreet banking task force.
jokes.setup: Selina's sexual euphemism for banking meetings
jokes.punchline: Gary earnestly offering to arrange what sounds like an affair
jokes.explanation: Gary's innocent response to what sounds increasingly like sexual arrangements

jokes.text: I don't want that kind of banking task force. I want the banking task force that I want.
jokes.setup: Gary offering discreet banking arrangements
jokes.punchline: Selina rejecting secretive meetings while sounding increasingly sexual
jokes.explanation: Continues the sexual subtext while maintaining plausible deniability

jokes.text: We got... votes.
jokes.setup: Amy trying to report recount results via bad video connection
jokes.punchline: Technical difficulties cutting out the crucial number
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy through technical failure at the worst possible moment

jokes.text: Oh, my God, this is my house. This is my house.
jokes.setup: Learning about the full state recount
jokes.punchline: Premature victory celebration claiming ownership
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's tendency to celebrate prematurely and claim territory

jokes.text: I'm feeling light-headed. That bottle's my only source of blood sugar.
jokes.setup: Kent holding Mike's cleanse bottle
jokes.punchline: Mike's dependence on his ridiculous diet
jokes.explanation: Continues Mike's health obsession storyline with pathetic dependency

jokes.text: Stop with the Kabuki show and eat a fucking cookie.
jokes.setup: Mike's dramatic health crisis over his cleanse
jokes.punchline: Ben's blunt assessment of Mike's theatrical dieting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben line cutting through Mike's pretensions with practical advice

jokes.text: Can you go tell him to fuck a bag of glass or something?
jokes.setup: Tom wanting to see her without appointment
jokes.punchline: Extremely violent and creative dismissal
jokes.explanation: Creative and violent imagery that's both shocking and oddly specific

jokes.text: So, economy czar, kids' birthday parties, reflecting pool boy.
jokes.setup: Tom offering to do whatever needed
jokes.punchline: Increasingly demeaning job titles
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Tom's willingness to debase himself. Strong structure.

jokes.text: Why did Tom James just wrap up his balls in butcher paper and put them in my purse?
jokes.setup: Tom's submissive behavior toward Selina
jokes.punchline: Graphic metaphor for emasculation and surrender
jokes.explanation: Vivid and specific imagery for political submission that's both funny and crude

jokes.text: We used to call that a Jimmy Carter.
jokes.setup: Selina saying she could shit in the Rose Garden
jokes.punchline: Suggesting this was named after a former president
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered throwaway line that implies a whole historical backstory

jokes.text: Come on, that's like saying that the cat doesn't like you or that table doesn't like you.
jokes.setup: Charlie thinking Gary doesn't like him
jokes.punchline: Comparing Gary to inanimate objects
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's view of Gary as basically furniture rather than a person

jokes.text: In another person it would be bragging, but with you it's something different.
jokes.setup: Charlie mentioning his multiple beach houses casually
jokes.punchline: Selina's backhanded compliment that's really about his wealth
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's attraction to wealth while pretending it's about character

jokes.text: Oh, that was a fuck, comma, today, yes!
jokes.setup: Continuing the ballot interpretation theme
jokes.punchline: Richard applying his comma theory to a different context
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Richard's consistent logical approach to profanity

jokes.text: You didn't tell me faggy hair was here.
jokes.setup: Sophie arriving unexpectedly and seeing Dan
jokes.punchline: Crude nickname for Dan delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Shows Sophie's crude character but relies on offensive language for impact

jokes.text: I thought that Carson City was, like, part of Las Vegas. Vegas is 432 miles away.
jokes.setup: Sophie complaining about Carson City
jokes.punchline: Her geographic ignorance and Dan's specific correction
jokes.explanation: Shows character contrast between Sophie's assumptions and Dan's precision

jokes.text: Sorry I'm not a 'where everything is' nerd.
jokes.setup: Dan correcting her geography
jokes.punchline: Sophie's dismissive term for basic geographic knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sophie line reducing geography to nerdiness rather than admitting ignorance

jokes.text: You know, I hardly ever did that with President Hughes. And even when we did, I was just kind of going through the motions.
jokes.setup: Talking about sex with Charlie
jokes.punchline: Comparing him favorably to her dead husband sexually
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate post-coital comparison that shows Selina's lack of filter

jokes.text: They call it the Pittsburgh of the South.
jokes.setup: Gary mentioning Birmingham is underrated
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment comparing it to another industrial city
jokes.explanation: Shows Charlie making polite conversation that's actually not very flattering

jokes.text: It's actually just me. I'm an only child.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking if Gary comes from a big family
jokes.punchline: Gary's enthusiastic agreement followed by contradiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment showing his eagerness to please even when making no sense

jokes.text: Well, what you have here is a classic two fires, one glove situation. And you need to know which hand to put the glove on.
jokes.setup: Asked about recount strategy
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical metaphor delivered with authority
jokes.explanation: Another brilliant Bradley non-sequitur that sounds wise but means nothing

jokes.text: No, oh, wait a minute. You mean Nevada. Nevada. What did I say? You said Nebraska.
jokes.setup: Bradley mentioning his flight destination
jokes.punchline: Confusion about which state they're actually fighting over
jokes.explanation: Shows Bradley's age and confusion but basic geographic mix-up

jokes.text: You know, I was over in the East Wing and I saw a painting of Sue holding George Washington's horse.
jokes.setup: Casual observation about White House art
jokes.punchline: Implying Sue is old enough to have been at the founding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant throwaway line about Sue's apparent immortality in the White House

jokes.text: He fucked me and then he fucked me? What, is he trying to fuck me?
jokes.setup: Learning Charlie went to O'Brien after sleeping with her
jokes.punchline: Triple use of 'fucked' in different contexts showing her rage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation using the same word in literal, metaphorical, and political contexts

jokes.text: Were you fantasizing I had some sort of a goatee and was on the wait list for a liver?
jokes.setup: Angry at Charlie for thinking about O'Brien meeting during sex
jokes.punchline: Specific and bizarre description of what O'Brien fantasy would involve
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd imagery that perfectly captures Selina's rage and creativity

jokes.text: Over? I didn't even know it was a thing. Absolutely not a thing. And if it was a thing, it's over.
jokes.setup: Selina declaring their relationship over
jokes.punchline: Charlie's contradictory statements about relationship status
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Charlie's desperate backtracking.

jokes.text: Uh, he's a Charlie Baird.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain Charlie's role at press conference
jokes.punchline: Completely meaningless circular definition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment of complete communication failure under pressure

jokes.text: Really? CBS? I would love to work at CBS.
jokes.setup: Sophie mentioning she works at CVS
jokes.punchline: Dan mishearing CVS as CBS and getting excited about media opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan moment showing his desperate careerism and selective hearing

tmdb_overview: Amy, Dan, Jonah and Richard try to win the Presidency for the President. Back in D.C., Selina brings in respected Washington fixture Bob Bradley, aka "The Eagle," and, after nudging out Tom James on the newly created Banking Task Force, meets billionaire banker Charlie Baird. Meanwhile, Mike tries to get healthy before his baby arrives; Kent investigates Sue's age; and Amy and Dan share a charged moment.
insight: Nevada campaign leg plants 81 jokes in 37 minutes, surviving political chaos on character comedy alone.
